zotero.abstractNote: We study the problem of lifelong learning of representations, where a learner encounters a sequence of tasks and aims to learn a shared representation that is useful for all tasks. We propose a simple algorithm based on alternating minimization and prove that it can learn a shared representation that is near-optimal for all tasks, provided the tasks share a common low-dimensional subspace. Our results provide a theoretical foundation for lifelong learning and demonstrate that it is possible to learn a shared representation with sample complexity that is significantly lower than learning each task independently.
zotero.conferenceName: 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics

extra.Editors: Camps-Valls, Gustau and Ruiz, Francisco J. R. and Valera, Isabel

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
